revision.comment: 新しいページ：粉糠山古墳（岐阜県大垣市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 粉糠山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
墳丘全景（左に後方部、右に前方部） |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[大垣市]]青墓町1 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 12.27 |経度度 =136|経度分 = 33|経度秒 = 53.29 |ISO = JP-21 |形状 = [[前方後方墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長100m
高さ6m（後方部） |築造年代 = [[4世紀]]末-[[5世紀]]初頭 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = |出土品 = 乳文鏡片・[[埴輪]]片 |史跡指定 = 大垣市指定史跡「粉糠山古墳」 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Gifu |ラベル = 粉糠山古墳 |アイコン = 前方後方墳-西 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''粉糠山古墳'''（こぬかやまこふん）は、[[岐阜県]][[大垣市]]青墓町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。大垣市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 岐阜県西部、大垣市街地から北西方の低位段丘上に築造された大型前方後方墳である{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。「粉糠山」の古墳名は、青墓宿の遊女が化粧に使用した粉糠を捨てて積もったことに由来するという史跡説明板。。現在の墳丘上は共同墓地として利用されているほか、考古学的にはこれまでに[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）に大垣市教育委員会による調査が実施されている{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。 墳形は前方後方形で、前方部を西方に向ける{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。墳丘は2段築成{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。墳丘長は100メートルを測るが、これは前方後方墳としては東海地方で最大規模になる{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]が認められているほか、墳丘周囲には周濠が巡らされている。主体部の埋葬施設は、削平のため詳らかでない{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。副葬品としては[[明治]]年間出土の乳文鏡片のみが知られる{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。以上より、築造年代は[[4世紀]]末-[[5世紀]]初頭頃と推定される。 古墳域は[[1967年]]（昭和42年）に大垣市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 規模 == [[File:Konukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|後方部墳頂}}]] * 墳丘長：100メートル * 後方部 ** 一辺：50メートル ** 高さ：6メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：45メートル ** 高さ：4メートル == 文化財 == === 大垣市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 粉糠山古墳 - 1967年（昭和42年）9月1日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf 指定文化財一覧表]}}（大垣市ホームページ、2017年2月22日版）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（大垣市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=中井正幸|year=1989|chapter=粉糠山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989年}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|title=粉糠山古墳 -範囲確認調査報告書-（大垣市埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第2集）|publisher=大垣市教育委員会文化部|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[昼飯大塚古墳]]（大垣市昼飯町） - 粉糠山古墳の東方約600メートルに所在。岐阜県で最大規模の古墳。 {{デフォルトソート:こぬかやまこふん}} [[Category:岐阜県の古墳]] [[Category:大垣市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 粉糠山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
墳丘全景（左に後方部、右に前方部） |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[大垣市]]青墓町1 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 12.27 |経度度 =136|経度分 = 33|経度秒 = 53.29 |ISO = JP-21 |形状 = [[前方後方墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長100m
高さ6m（後方部） |築造年代 = [[4世紀]]末-[[5世紀]]初頭 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = |出土品 = 乳文鏡片・[[埴輪]]片 |史跡指定 = 大垣市指定史跡「粉糠山古墳」 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Gifu |ラベル = 粉糠山古墳 |アイコン = 前方後方墳-西 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''粉糠山古墳'''（こぬかやまこふん）は、[[岐阜県]][[大垣市]]青墓町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。大垣市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 岐阜県西部、大垣市街地から北西方の低位段丘上に築造された大型前方後方墳である{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。「粉糠山」の古墳名は、青墓宿の遊女が化粧に使用した粉糠を捨てて積もったことに由来するという史跡説明板。。現在の墳丘上は共同墓地として利用されているほか、考古学的にはこれまでに[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）に大垣市教育委員会による調査が実施されている{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。 墳形は前方後方形で、前方部を西方に向ける{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。墳丘は2段築成{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。墳丘長は100メートルを測るが、これは前方後方墳としては東海地方で最大規模になる{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]が認められているほか、墳丘周囲には周濠が巡らされている。主体部の埋葬施設は、削平のため詳らかでない{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。副葬品としては[[明治]]年間出土の乳文鏡片のみが知られる{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。以上より、築造年代は[[古墳時代]]前期末-中期初頭の[[4世紀]]末-[[5世紀]]初頭頃と推定される。 古墳域は[[1967年]]（昭和42年）に大垣市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 規模 == [[File:Konukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|後方部墳頂}}]] * 墳丘長：100メートル * 後方部 ** 一辺：50メートル ** 高さ：6メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：45メートル ** 高さ：4メートル == 文化財 == === 大垣市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 粉糠山古墳 - 1967年（昭和42年）9月1日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf 指定文化財一覧表]}}（大垣市ホームページ、2017年2月22日版）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（大垣市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=中井正幸|year=1989|chapter=粉糠山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989年}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|title=粉糠山古墳 -範囲確認調査報告書-（大垣市埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第2集）|publisher=大垣市教育委員会文化部|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[昼飯大塚古墳]]（大垣市昼飯町） - 粉糠山古墳の東方約600メートルに所在。岐阜県で最大規模の古墳。 {{デフォルトソート:こぬかやまこふん}} [[Category:岐阜県の古墳]] [[Category:大垣市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]]

墳丘全景（左に後方部、右に前方部） |別名 = |所属 = 不破古墳群 |所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[大垣市]]青墓町1 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 12.27 |経度度 =136|経度分 = 33|経度秒 = 53.29 |ISO = JP-21 |形状 = [[前方後方墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長100m
高さ6m（後方部） |築造年代 = [[4世紀]]末-[[5世紀]]初頭 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = |出土品 = 乳文鏡片・[[埴輪]]片 |史跡指定 = 大垣市指定史跡「粉糠山古墳」 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Gifu |ラベル = 粉糠山古墳 |アイコン = 前方後方墳-西 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''粉糠山古墳'''（こぬかやまこふん）は、[[岐阜県]][[大垣市]]青墓町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。不破古墳群を構成する古墳の1つ。大垣市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+不破古墳群の盟主的首長墓系譜中井正幸 『昼飯大塚古墳 -美濃最大の前方後円墳-（日本の遺跡 21）』 同成社、2007年、pp. 133-147。 !造墓基盤!!古墳名!!形状!!墳丘長 |- |昼飯||花岡山古墳||[[前方後円墳]]||60m |- |垂井北群||親ヶ谷古墳||前方後円墳||85m以上 |- |矢道||矢道長塚古墳||前方後円墳||90m |- |青墓||'''粉糠山古墳'''||[[前方後方墳]]||100m |- |昼飯||[[昼飯大塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||150m |- |青墓||遊塚古墳||前方後円墳||80m |}
岐阜県西部、大垣市街地から北西方の低位段丘上に築造された大型前方後方墳である{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。「粉糠山」の古墳名は、青墓宿の遊女が化粧に使用した粉糠を捨てて積もったことに由来するという史跡説明板。。現在の墳丘上は共同墓地として利用されているほか、考古学的にはこれまでに[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）に大垣市教育委員会による調査が実施されている{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。 墳形は前方後方形で、前方部を西方に向ける{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。墳丘は2段築成{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。墳丘長は100メートルを測るが、これは前方後方墳としては東海地方で最大規模になる{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]が認められているほか、墳丘周囲には周濠が巡らされている。主体部の埋葬施設は、削平のため詳らかでない{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。副葬品としては[[明治]]年間出土の乳文鏡片のみが知られる{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989年}}。以上より、築造年代は[[古墳時代]]中期の[[4世紀]]末-[[5世紀]]初頭頃と推定される。付近では岐阜県最大規模の[[前方後円墳]]である[[昼飯大塚古墳]]（大垣市昼飯町、150メートル）の築造が知られ、粉糠山古墳は昼飯大塚古墳と近接する時期の築造とされるが、両古墳の墳形が異なる点、および両古墳の間には谷が存在して造墓基盤勢力も異なる点が注目される中井正幸 『昼飯大塚古墳 -美濃最大の前方後円墳-（日本の遺跡 21）』 同成社、2007年、p. 12。。 古墳域は[[1967年]]（昭和42年）に大垣市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 規模 == [[File:Konukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|後方部墳頂}}]] * 墳丘長：100メートル * 後方部 ** 一辺：50メートル ** 高さ：6メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：45メートル ** 高さ：4メートル == 文化財 == === 大垣市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 粉糠山古墳 - 1967年（昭和42年）9月1日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf 指定文化財一覧表]}}（大垣市ホームページ、2017年2月22日版）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（大垣市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=中井正幸|year=1989|chapter=粉糠山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989年}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|title=粉糠山古墳 -範囲確認調査報告書-（大垣市埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第2集）|publisher=大垣市教育委員会文化部|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[昼飯大塚古墳]]（大垣市昼飯町） - 粉糠山古墳の東方約600メートルに所在。岐阜県で最大規模の古墳。 {{デフォルトソート:こぬかやまこふん}} [[Category:岐阜県の古墳]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:大垣市の歴史]]

岐阜県西部、大垣市街地から北西方の低位段丘上に築造された大型前方後方墳である{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。「粉糠山」の古墳名は、青墓宿の遊女が化粧に使用した粉糠を捨てて積もったことに由来するという史跡説明板。。現在の墳丘上は共同墓地として利用されているほか、考古学的にはこれまでに[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）に大垣市教育委員会による調査が実施されている{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 墳形は前方後方形で、前方部を西方に向ける{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘は2段築成{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘長は100メートルを測り、前方後方墳としては東海地方で最大規模になる{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]が認められているほか、墳丘周囲には周濠が巡らされている。埋葬施設は、削平のため詳らかでない{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。副葬品としては[[明治]]年間出土の乳文鏡片のみが知られる{{Sfn|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。以上より、築造時期は[[古墳時代]]中期の[[4世紀]]末-[[5世紀]]初頭頃と推定される。付近では岐阜県最大規模の[[前方後円墳]]である[[昼飯大塚古墳]]（大垣市昼飯町、150メートル）の築造が知られ、粉糠山古墳は昼飯大塚古墳と近接する時期の築造とされるが、両古墳の墳形が異なる点、および両古墳の間には谷が存在して造墓基盤勢力も異なる点が注目される中井正幸 『昼飯大塚古墳 -美濃最大の前方後円墳-（日本の遺跡 21）』 同成社、2007年、p. 12。。 古墳域は[[1967年]]（昭和42年）に大垣市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 構造 == [[File:Konukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|後方部墳頂}}]] * 墳丘長：100メートル * 後方部 ** 一辺：50メートル ** 高さ：6メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：45メートル ** 高さ：4メートル == 文化財 == === 大垣市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 粉糠山古墳 - 1967年（昭和42年）9月1日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf 指定文化財一覧表]}}（大垣市ホームページ、2017年2月22日版）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（大垣市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=中井正幸|year=1989|chapter=粉糠山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|粉糠山古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|title=粉糠山古墳 -範囲確認調査報告書-（大垣市埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第2集）|publisher=大垣市教育委員会文化部|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[昼飯大塚古墳]]（大垣市昼飯町） - 粉糠山古墳の東方約600メートルに所在。岐阜県で最大規模の古墳。 {{デフォルトソート:こぬかやまこふん}} [[Category:岐阜県の古墳]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:大垣市の歴史]]

{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+不破古墳群の盟主的首長墓系譜中井正幸 『昼飯大塚古墳 -美濃最大の前方後円墳-（日本の遺跡 21）』 同成社、2007年、pp. 133-147。 !造墓基盤!!古墳名!!形状!!墳丘長 |- |昼飯||花岡山古墳||[[前方後円墳]]||60m |- |垂井北群||[[親ヶ谷古墳]]||前方後円墳||85m以上 |- |矢道||矢道長塚古墳||前方後円墳||90m |- |青墓||'''粉糠山古墳'''||[[前方後方墳]]||100m |- |昼飯||[[昼飯大塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||150m |- |青墓||遊塚古墳||前方後円墳||80m |}

revision.text:
{{Kofun of Japan |Name = Konukayama Kofun |Image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |Location = {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan |Coordinates = {{coord|35|23|12.27|N|136|33|53.29|E|region:JP-21_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Konukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} |Size = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |Era = Late 4th to early 5th century |Burial = Unknown |Artifacts = Mirror fragments, haniwa |Historic Designation = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |Map = Japan Gifu |Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |Label = Konukayama Kofun |Label Position = top }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群}} and is a designated historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic site information board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== Structure === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * **Posterior mound:** * Width: 50 meters * Height: 6 meters * **Anterior mound:** * Width: 45 meters * Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== Artifacts === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a {{ill|乳文鏡|ja|}} (breast-shaped pattern mirror), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== Historic Designation == The site was designated a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of Burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Cultural Properties Guide]
{{DEFAULTSORT:Konukayama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Ōgaki City Designated Cultural Properties]] [[Category:History of Ōgaki]]

== References == {{reflist}}

== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Cultural Properties Guide]

These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== Historic Designation == The site was designated a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of Burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Cultural Properties Guide]

'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群}} and is a designated historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic site information board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== Structure === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * **Posterior mound:** * Width: 50 meters * Height: 6 meters * **Anterior mound:** * Width: 45 meters * Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== Artifacts === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a {{ill|乳文鏡|ja|}} (breast-shaped pattern mirror), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== Historic Designation == The site was designated a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>

'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群}} and is a designated historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic site information board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== Structure === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ** Width: 50 meters ** Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ** Width: 45 meters ** Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== Artifacts === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a {{ill|乳文鏡|ja|}} (breast-shaped pattern mirror), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== Historic Designation == The site was designated a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.

== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|23|12.27|N|136|33|53.29|E|region:JP-21_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Konukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} |Size = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |Era = Late 4th to early 5th century |Burial = Unknown |Artifacts = Mirror fragments, haniwa |Historic Designation = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |Map = Japan Gifu |Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |Label = Konukayama Kofun |Label Position = top }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群}} and is a designated historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic site information board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== Structure === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ** Width: 50 meters ** Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ** Width: 45 meters ** Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== Artifacts === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== Historic Designation == The site was designated a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of Burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Cultural Properties Guide]

'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群}} and is a designated historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic site information board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== Structure === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ** Width: 50 meters ** Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ** Width: 45 meters ** Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== Artifacts === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== Historic Designation == The site was designated a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of Burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Cultural Properties Guide]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|23|12.27|N|136|33|53.29|E|region:JP-21_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Konukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} |Size = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |Era = Late 4th to early 5th century |Burial = Unknown |Artifacts = Mirror fragments, haniwa |Historic Designation = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |Map = Japan Gifu |Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |Label = Konukayama Kofun |Label Position = top }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群}} and is a designated historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic site information board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== Structure === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ** Width: 50 meters ** Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ** Width: 45 meters ** Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== Artifacts === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== Historic Designation == The site was designated a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of Burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Cultural Properties Guide]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|23|12.27|N|136|33|53.29|E|region:JP-21_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Konukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} |Size = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |Era = Late 4th to early 5th century |Burial = Unknown |Artifacts = Mirror fragments, haniwa |Historic Designation = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |Map = Japan Gifu |Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |Label = Konukayama Kofun |Label Position = top }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群}} and is a designated historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic site information board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== Structure === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ** Width: 50 meters ** Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ** Width: 45 meters ** Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== Artifacts === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== Historic Designation == The site was designated a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of Burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Cultural Properties Guide]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|23|12.27|N|136|33|53.29|E|region:JP-21_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Konukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} |Size = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |Era = Late 4th to early 5th century |Burial = Unknown |Artifacts = Mirror fragments, haniwa |Historic Designation = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |Map = Japan Gifu |Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |Label = Konukayama Kofun |Label Position = top }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群}} and is a designated historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic site information board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== Structure === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ** Width: 50 meters ** Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ** Width: 45 meters ** Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== Artifacts === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== Historic Designation == The site was designated a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of Burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Cultural Properties Guide]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|23|12.27|N|136|33|53.29|E|region:JP-21_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Konukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} |Size = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |Era = Late 4th to early 5th century |Burial = Unknown |Artifacts = Mirror fragments, haniwa |Historic Designation = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |Map = Japan Gifu |Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |Label = Konukayama Kofun |Label Position = top }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群}} and is a designated historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic site information board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== Structure === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ** Width: 50 meters ** Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ** Width: 45 meters ** Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== Artifacts === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== Historic Designation == The site was designated a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of Burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Cultural Properties Guide]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|23|12.27|N|136|33|53.29|E|region:JP-21_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Konukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} |Size = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |Era = Late 4th to early 5th century |Burial = Unknown |Artifacts = Mirror fragments, haniwa |Historic Designation = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |Map = Japan Gifu |Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |Label = Konukayama Kofun |Label Position = top }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群}} and is a designated historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic site information board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== Structure === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ** Width: 50 meters ** Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ** Width: 45 meters ** Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== Artifacts === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== Historic Designation == The site was designated a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of Burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Cultural Properties Guide]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|23|12.27|N|136|33|53.29|E|region:JP-21_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Konukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} |Size = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |Era = Late 4th to early 5th century |Burial = Unknown |Artifacts = Mirror fragments, haniwa |Historic Designation = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |Map = Japan Gifu |Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |Label = Konukayama Kofun |Label Position = top }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群}} and is a designated historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic site information board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== Structure === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ** Width: 50 meters ** Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ** Width: 45 meters ** Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== Artifacts === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== Historic Designation == The site was designated a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of Burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Cultural Properties Guide]

== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== Historic Designation == The site was designated a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of Burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Cultural Properties Guide]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|23|12.27|N|136|33|53.29|E|region:JP-21_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Konukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} |Size = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |Era = Late 4th to early 5th century |Burial = Unknown |Artifacts = Mirror fragments, haniwa |Historic Designation = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |Map = Japan Gifu |Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |Label = Konukayama Kofun |Label Position = top }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群}} and is a designated historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic site information board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== Structure === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ** Width: 50 meters ** Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ** Width: 45 meters ** Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== Artifacts === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== Historic Designation == The site was designated a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of Burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Cultural Properties Guide]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|23|12.27|N|136|33|53.29|E|region:JP-21_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Konukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} |Size = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |Era = Late 4th to early 5th century |Burial = Unknown |Artifacts = Mirror fragments, haniwa |Historic Designation = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |Map = Japan Gifu |Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |Label = Konukayama Kofun |Label Position = top }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群}} and is a designated historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic site information board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== Structure === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ** Width: 50 meters ** Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ** Width: 45 meters ** Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== Artifacts === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== Historic Designation == The site was designated a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of Burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Cultural Properties Guide]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|23|12.27|N|136|33|53.29|E|region:JP-21_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Konukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} |Size = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |Era = Late 4th to early 5th century |Burial = Unknown |Artifacts = Mirror fragments, haniwa |Historic Designation = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |Map = Japan Gifu |Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |Label = Konukayama Kofun |Label Position = top }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群}} and is a designated historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic site information board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== Structure === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ** Width: 50 meters ** Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ** Width: 45 meters ** Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== Artifacts === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== Historic Designation == The site was designated a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of Burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Cultural Properties Guide]

'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群}} and is a designated historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic site information board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== Structure === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ** Width: 50 meters ** Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ** Width: 45 meters ** Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== Artifacts === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== Historic Designation == The site was designated a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of Burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Cultural Properties Guide]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|23|12.27|N|136|33|53.29|E|region:JP-21_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Konukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} |Size = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |Era = Late 4th to early 5th century |Burial = Unknown |Artifacts = Mirror fragments, haniwa |Historic Designation = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |Map = Japan Gifu |Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |Label = Konukayama Kofun |Label Position = top }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群}} and is a designated historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic site information board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== Structure === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ''' Width: 50 meters ''' Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ''' Width: 45 meters ''' Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== Artifacts === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== Historic Designation == The site was designated a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of Burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Cultural Properties Guide]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|23|12.27|N|136|33|53.29|E|region:JP-21_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Konukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} |Size = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |Era = Late 4th to early 5th century |Burial = Unknown |Artifacts = Mirror fragments, haniwa |Historic Designation = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |Map = Japan Gifu |Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |Label = Konukayama Kofun |Label Position = top }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群}} and is a designated historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic site information board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== Structure === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ''' Width: 50 meters ''' Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ''' Width: 45 meters ''' Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== Artifacts === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== Historic Designation == The site was designated a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of Burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Cultural Properties Guide]

The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== Artifacts === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== Historic Designation == The site was designated a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of Burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Cultural Properties Guide]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|23|12.27|N|136|33|53.29|E|region:JP-21_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Konukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} |Size = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |Era = Late 4th to early 5th century |Burial = Unknown |Artifacts = Mirror fragments, haniwa |Historic Designation = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |Map = Japan Gifu |Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |Label = Konukayama Kofun |Label Position = top }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群}} and is a designated historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic site information board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== Structure === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ''' Width: 50 meters ''' Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ''' Width: 45 meters ''' Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== Artifacts === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== Historic Designation == The site was designated a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of Burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Cultural Properties Guide]

== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後方墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|23|12.27|N|136|33|53.29|E|region:JP-21_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Konukayama Kofun}} |Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} |Size = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |Era = Late 4th to early 5th century |Burial = Unknown |Artifacts = Mirror fragments, haniwa |Historic Designation = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |Map = Japan Gifu |Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |Label = Konukayama Kofun |Label Position = top }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群}} and is a designated historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic site information board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== Structure === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ''' Width: 50 meters ''' Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ''' Width: 45 meters ''' Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== Artifacts === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== Historic Designation == The site was designated a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of Burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Cultural Properties Guide]

== Historic Designation == The site was designated a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of Burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Cultural Properties Guide]
