So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft}} |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|23|12.27|N|136|33|53.29|E|region:JP-21_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Konukayama Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft}} |area = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |built = Late 4th to early 5th century |burial = Unknown |burial goods = Mirror fragments, haniwa |alternate_name = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |map = Japan Gifu |type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |map_dot_label = Konukayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群|qq=draft}} and is a Historic Site1 historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic Site intypeation board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== burial === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ''' Width: 50 meters ''' Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ''' Width: 45 meters ''' Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== burial goods === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳|qq=draft}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== alternate_name == The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Historic Site]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳|qq=draft}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Historic Site Guide]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft}} |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|23|12.27|N|136|33|53.29|E|region:JP-21_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Konukayama Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft}} |area = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |built = Late 4th to early 5th century |burial = Unknown |burial goods = Mirror fragments, haniwa |alternate_name = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |map = Japan Gifu |type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |map_dot_label = Konukayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群|qq=draft}} and is a Historic Site1 historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic Site intypeation board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== burial === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ''' Width: 50 meters ''' Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ''' Width: 45 meters ''' Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== burial goods === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳|qq=draft}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== alternate_name == The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Historic Site]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳|qq=draft}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Historic Site Guide]

It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== alternate_name == The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Historic Site]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Historic Site Guide]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|23|12.27|N|136|33|53.29|E|region:JP-21_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Konukayama Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} |area = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |built = Late 4th to early 5th century |burial = Unknown |burial goods = Mirror fragments, haniwa |alternate_name = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |map = Japan Gifu |type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |map_dot_label = Konukayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ōgaki|ja|大垣市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwa Kofun Group|qq=}} and is a Historic Site1 historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic Site intypeation board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== burial === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ''' Width: 50 meters ''' Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ''' Width: 45 meters ''' Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== burial goods === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/乳文鏡|qq=}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|lt=Hiruiōzuka Kofun|lt=Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== alternate_name == The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Historic Site]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Historic Site Guide]

It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|lt=Hiruiōzuka Kofun|lt=Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== alternate_name == The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Historic Site]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = [[:en:Ōgaki|Ōgaki]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|23|12.27|N|136|33|53.29|E|region:JP-21_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Konukayama Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} |area = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |built = Late 4th to early 5th century |burial = Unknown |burial goods = Mirror fragments, haniwa |alternate_name = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |map = Japan Gifu |type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |map_dot_label = Konukayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Ōgaki|Ōgaki]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwa Kofun Group|qq=}} and is a Historic Site1 historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic Site intypeation board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== burial === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ''' Width: 50 meters ''' Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ''' Width: 45 meters ''' Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== burial goods === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/乳文鏡|qq=}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger [[:en:Hiruiōzuka Kofun|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun]], located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== alternate_name == The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Historic Site]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * [[:en:Hiruiōzuka Kofun|Hiruiōzuka Kofun]] (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Historic Site Guide]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = [[:en:Ōgaki|Ōgaki]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|23|12.27|N|136|33|53.29|E|region:JP-21_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Konukayama Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} |area = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |built = Late 4th to early 5th century |burial = Unknown |burial goods = Mirror fragments, haniwa |alternate_name = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |map = Japan Gifu |type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |map_dot_label = Konukayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Ōgaki|Ōgaki]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwa Kofun Group|qq=}} and is a Historic Site1 historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic Site intypeation board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== burial === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ''' Width: 50 meters ''' Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ''' Width: 45 meters ''' Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== burial goods === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/乳文鏡|qq=}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger [[:en:Hiruiōzuka Kofun|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun]], located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== alternate_name == The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Historic Site]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * [[:en:Hiruiōzuka Kofun|Hiruiōzuka Kofun]] (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Historic Site Guide]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = [[:en:Ōgaki|Ōgaki]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|23|12.27|N|136|33|53.29|E|region:JP-21_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Konukayama Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} |area = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |built = Late 4th to early 5th century |burial = Unknown |burial goods = Mirror fragments, haniwa |alternate_name = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |map = Japan Gifu |type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |map_dot_label = Konukayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Ōgaki|Ōgaki]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwa Kofun Group|qq=}} and is a Historic Site1 historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic Site intypeation board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== burial === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ''' Width: 50 meters ''' Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ''' Width: 45 meters ''' Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== burial goods === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/乳文鏡|qq=}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger [[:en:Hiruiōzuka Kofun|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun]], located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== alternate_name == The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Historic Site]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * [[:en:Hiruiōzuka Kofun|Hiruiōzuka Kofun]] (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Historic Site Guide]

'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Ōgaki|Ōgaki]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwa Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is a Historic Site1 historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic Site intypeation board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== burial === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ''' Width: 50 meters ''' Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ''' Width: 45 meters ''' Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== burial goods === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/乳文鏡|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger [[:en:Hiruiōzuka Kofun|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun]], located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== alternate_name == The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Historic Site]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * [[:en:Hiruiōzuka Kofun|Hiruiōzuka Kofun]] (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Historic Site Guide]

'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Ōgaki|Ōgaki]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwa Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is a Historic Site1 historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic Site intypeation board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== burial === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ''' Width: 50 meters ''' Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ''' Width: 45 meters ''' Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== burial goods === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/乳文鏡|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger [[:en:Hiruiōzuka Kofun|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun]], located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== alternate_name == The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Historic Site]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * [[:en:Hiruiōzuka Kofun|Hiruiōzuka Kofun]] (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Historic Site Guide]

'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Ōgaki|Ōgaki]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwa Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is a Historic Site1 historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic Site intypeation board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== burial === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ''' Width: 50 meters ''' Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ''' Width: 45 meters ''' Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== burial goods === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/乳文鏡|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger [[:en:Hiruiōzuka Kofun|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun]], located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== alternate_name == The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Historic Site]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * [[:en:Hiruiōzuka Kofun|Hiruiōzuka Kofun]] (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Historic Site Guide]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = [[:en:Ōgaki|Ōgaki]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|35.386742|N|136.564803|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |built = Late 4th to early 5th century |burial = Unknown |burial goods = Mirror fragments, haniwa |alternate_name = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |map = Japan Gifu |type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |map_dot_label = Konukayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Ōgaki|Ōgaki]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwa Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is a Historic Site1 historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic Site intypeation board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== burial === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ''' Width: 50 meters ''' Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ''' Width: 45 meters ''' Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== burial goods === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/乳文鏡|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger [[:en:Hiruiōzuka Kofun|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun]], located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== alternate_name == The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Historic Site]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * [[:en:Hiruiōzuka Kofun|Hiruiōzuka Kofun]] (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Historic Site Guide]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = [[:en:Ōgaki|Ōgaki]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|35.386742|N|136.564803|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |built = Late 4th to early 5th century |burial = Unknown |burial goods = Mirror fragments, haniwa |alternate_name = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |map = Japan Gifu |type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |map_dot_label = Konukayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Ōgaki|Ōgaki]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwa Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is a Historic Site1 historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic Site intypeation board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== burial === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ''' Width: 50 meters ''' Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ''' Width: 45 meters ''' Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== burial goods === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/乳文鏡|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger [[:en:Hiruiōzuka Kofun|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun]], located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== alternate_name == The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Historic Site]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * [[:en:Hiruiōzuka Kofun|Hiruiōzuka Kofun]] (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Historic Site Guide]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = [[:en:Ōgaki|Ōgaki]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|35.386742|N|136.564803|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |built = Late 4th to early 5th century |burial = Unknown |burial goods = Mirror fragments, haniwa |alternate_name = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |map = Japan Gifu |type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |map_dot_label = Konukayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Ōgaki|Ōgaki]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwa Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is a Historic Site1 historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic Site intypeation board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== burial === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ''' Width: 50 meters ''' Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ''' Width: 45 meters ''' Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== burial goods === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/乳文鏡|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger [[:en:Hiruiōzuka Kofun|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun]], located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== alternate_name == The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Historic Site]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * [[:en:Hiruiōzuka Kofun|Hiruiōzuka Kofun]] (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Historic Site Guide]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = [[:en:Ōgaki|Ōgaki]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|35.386742|N|136.564803|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |built = Late 4th to early 5th century |burial = Unknown |burial goods = Mirror fragments, haniwa |alternate_name = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |map = Japan Gifu |type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |map_dot_label = Konukayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Ōgaki|Ōgaki]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwa Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is a Historic Site1 historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic Site intypeation board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== burial === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ''' Width: 50 meters ''' Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ''' Width: 45 meters ''' Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== burial goods === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/乳文鏡|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger [[:en:Hiruiōzuka Kofun|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun]], located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== alternate_name == The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Historic Site]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * [[:en:Hiruiōzuka Kofun|Hiruiōzuka Kofun]] (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Historic Site Guide]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = [[:en:Ōgaki|Ōgaki]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|35.386742|N|136.564803|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |built = Late 4th to early 5th century |burial = Unknown |burial goods = Mirror fragments, haniwa |alternate_name = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |map = Japan Gifu |type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |map_dot_label = Konukayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Ōgaki|Ōgaki]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwa Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is a Historic Site1 historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic Site intypeation board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== burial === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ''' Width: 50 meters ''' Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ''' Width: 45 meters ''' Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== burial goods === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/乳文鏡|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger [[:en:Hiruiōzuka Kofun|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun]], located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== alternate_name == The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Historic Site]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * [[:en:Hiruiōzuka Kofun|Hiruiōzuka Kofun]] (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Historic Site Guide]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = [[:en:Ōgaki|Ōgaki]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|35.386742|N|136.564803|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |built = Late 4th to early 5th century |burial = Unknown |burial goods = Mirror fragments, haniwa |alternate_name = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |map = Japan Gifu |type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |map_dot_label = Konukayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Ōgaki|Ōgaki]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwa Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is a Historic Site1 historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic Site intypeation board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== burial === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ''' Width: 50 meters ''' Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ''' Width: 45 meters ''' Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== burial goods === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/乳文鏡|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger [[:en:Hiruiōzuka Kofun|Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun]], located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== alternate_name == The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Historic Site]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * [[:en:Hiruiōzuka Kofun|Hiruiōzuka Kofun]] (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Historic Site Guide]
