{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = {{ill|Ōgaki|en|Ōgaki|WD=Q384926|lt=Ōgaki}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|35.386742|N|136.564803|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |built = Late 4th to early 5th century |burial = Unknown |burial goods = Mirror fragments, haniwa |alternate_name = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |map = Japan Gifu |type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |map_dot_label = Konukayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|Ōgaki|WD=Q384926|lt=Ōgaki}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwa Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is a Historic Site1 historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic Site intypeation board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== burial === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ''' Width: 50 meters ''' Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ''' Width: 45 meters ''' Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== burial goods === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/乳文鏡|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|WD=Q11514249|lt=Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== alternate_name == The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Historic Site]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|WD=Q11514249|lt=Hiruiōzuka Kofun}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Historic Site Guide]

The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== burial goods === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/乳文鏡|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|WD=Q11514249|lt=Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== alternate_name == The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Historic Site]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|WD=Q11514249|lt=Hiruiōzuka Kofun}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Historic Site Guide]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = {{ill|Ōgaki|en|Ōgaki|WD=Q384926|lt=Ōgaki|1=Ōgaki}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|35.386742|N|136.564803|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |built = Late 4th to early 5th century |burial = Unknown |burial goods = Mirror fragments, haniwa |alternate_name = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |map = Japan Gifu |type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |map_dot_label = Konukayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|Ōgaki|WD=Q384926|lt=Ōgaki|1=Ōgaki}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwa Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is a Historic Site1 historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic Site intypeation board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== burial === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ''' Width: 50 meters ''' Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ''' Width: 45 meters ''' Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== burial goods === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/乳文鏡|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|WD=Q11514249|lt=Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|1=Hiruiōzuka Kofun}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== alternate_name == The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Historic Site]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|WD=Q11514249|lt=Hiruiōzuka Kofun|1=Hiruiōzuka Kofun}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Historic Site Guide]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Konukayama Kofun |image = [[File:Konukayama Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound overview (left: posterior, right: anterior) |location = {{ill|Ōgaki|en|Ōgaki|qid=Q384926|lt=Ōgaki|1=Ōgaki}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|35.386742|N|136.564803|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Total length: 100m, height: 6m (posterior mound) |built = Late 4th to early 5th century |burial = Unknown |burial goods = Mirror fragments, haniwa |alternate_name = Ōgaki City Designated Historic Site (since 1967) |map = Japan Gifu |type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-W |map_dot_label = Konukayama Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qid=Q11397225|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} }}
'''Konukayama Kofun''' (粉糠山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|Ōgaki|qid=Q384926|lt=Ōgaki|1=Ōgaki}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Fuwa Kofun Group|ja|不破古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwa Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is a Historic Site1 historic site of Ōgaki City.
== Overview == Situated on a low terrace northwest of central Ōgaki City, Konukayama Kofun is one of the largest {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Tōkai region, measuring 100 meters in total length. The name "Konukayama" originates from a local legend about the powdered cosmetics (粉糠) discarded by courtesans in the nearby Aonuma area.<ref name="description">Historic Site intypeation board by Ōgaki City Board of Education.</ref>
Currently, the mound serves as a community burial ground. Archaeological surveys have been conducted, notably in 1987 by the Ōgaki City Board of Education.<ref name="description" />
=== burial === The mound features a typical two-tier construction: * '''Posterior mound:''' ''' Width: 50 meters ''' Height: 6 meters * '''Anterior mound:''' ''' Width: 45 meters ''' Height: 4 meters
The mound is surrounded by a moat and features surface decorations of fukiishi (paving stones) and haniwa fragments. However, the burial facility remains unconfirmed due to degradation.<ref name="description" />
=== burial goods === The primary artifact discovered is a fragment of a breast-shaped pattern mirror ({{ill|乳文鏡|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/乳文鏡|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), along with fragments of haniwa. These findings date the tumulus to the late 4th to early 5th century, consistent with the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}.<ref name="description" />
== Cultural Significance == Konukayama Kofun is notable for its size and location within the Fuwa Kofun Group. It is often compared to the larger {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qid=Q11514249|lt=Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun|1=Hiruiōzuka Kofun}}, located 600 meters to the east. Despite their proximity, differences in burial practices and construction suggest distinct local power bases during their respective periods.<ref>Nakai, Masayuki, ''Hiruha Ōtsuka Kofun - Largest Zenpō-Kōen-Fun in Mino'' (2007).</ref>
== alternate_name == The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Ōgaki City in 1967.<ref name="cultural_list">{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000012/12659/bunkazai-ichiran290222.pdf List of Designated Historic Site]}} (Ōgaki City website, 2017).</ref>
== Access == The site is accessible from Ōgaki City Center via local transport and is situated in a residential area of Aonuma.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qid=Q11514249|lt=Hiruiōzuka Kofun|1=Hiruiōzuka Kofun}} (150 meters long) - The largest zenpō-kōen-fun in Gifu Prefecture, located approximately 600 meters to the east.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Nakai, Masayuki. ''Konukayama Kofun - Verification of burial Practices in the Fuwa Region.'' Ōgaki City Board of Education Report (1989).
== External Links == * [https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/ Ōgaki City Historic Site Guide]
